jokes.text: We think it's a sinkhole! It's awesome! / It's really not that awesome.
jokes.setup: Mac's excited announcement about finding a sinkhole
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate deflation of Mac's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis deflating Mac's childlike enthusiasm. Good character work and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We think it's a sinkhole! It's awesome!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's really not that awesome.

jokes.text: Look, I was a little bit skeptical at first, too, but we've been playing with this thing for three hours now, and it's fantastic.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to convince Dennis the hole is amazing
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of grown men playing with a hole for three hours
jokes.explanation: Mac's earnest defense of playing with a hole is perfectly childish and absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Look, I was a little bit skeptical at first, too, but we've been playing with this thing for three hours now, and it's fantastic.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, can I ask how do you play with a goddamn hole?
jokes.setup: Mac's claim about playing with the hole
jokes.punchline: Dennis asking the obvious question about how one 'plays' with a hole
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Dennis being the voice of reason while everyone else is insane.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, can I ask how do you play with a goddamn hole?

jokes.text: Then I threw it down there, you know, to prove a point. / What was the point? / It was something to do with how metal travels faster than light or...?
jokes.setup: Charlie claiming he had a scientific point
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely wrong pseudo-scientific reasoning
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie pseudo-science. His confident delivery of completely wrong information is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Then I threw it down there, you know, to prove a point.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What was the point?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It was something to do with how metal travels faster than light or...?

jokes.text: No, it was that light is either on or off. There's no speed to light. It's, it was an anti-science thing.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to explain his scientific reasoning
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete misunderstanding of physics delivered as 'anti-science'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Charlie moment. The idea that he's anti-science while trying to do science is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, it was that light is either on or off. There's no speed to light. It's, it was an anti-science thing.

jokes.text: What if there's, like, a mutant living down there? We can get him up and he can live in the bar with us.
jokes.setup: Discussing possibilities of what's in the hole
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate jump to wanting to adopt a hypothetical mutant
jokes.explanation: Mac's childlike enthusiasm for adopting a sewer mutant is perfectly absurd and in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What if there's, like, a mutant living down there? We can get him up and he can live in the bar with us.

jokes.text: We've already got one mutant hanging out with us in the bar all the time.
jokes.setup: Mac's suggestion to adopt a mutant
jokes.punchline: Dennis pointing out they already have a mutant (presumably Frank)
jokes.explanation: Sharp callback and insult. Perfect economy and Dennis's casual cruelty.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We've already got one mutant hanging out with us in the bar all the time.

jokes.text: Hey, Frank, did you hear what I said? I turned a frustrating conversation into a joke on you.
jokes.setup: Dennis making the mutant joke about Frank
jokes.punchline: Dennis explicitly explaining his own joke to Frank
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about explaining the joke. Dennis's narcissistic need for recognition is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Hey, Frank, did you hear what I said? I turned a frustrating conversation into a joke on you.

jokes.text: The, uh... thing. / Huh? Oh, y-yeah.
jokes.setup: Dennis waiting for Frank's reaction to his joke
jokes.punchline: Frank's complete non-reaction and confusion
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dennis's ego. Frank not paying attention undercuts Dennis's self-congratulation.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: The, uh... thing.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Huh? Oh, y-yeah.

jokes.text: No. I don't. In fact, something just dawned on me. I need to quit. / I hereby officially retire from Paddy's Pub.
jokes.setup: Simple question about the hole
jokes.punchline: Frank's dramatic leap from disinterest to retirement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank escalation. Going from hole disinterest to full retirement is beautifully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No. I don't. In fact, something just dawned on me. I need to quit. / I hereby officially retire from Paddy's Pub.

jokes.text: Frank owned 51% of Paddy's Pub and we own 46%. Well, we did, not-not Dee. She-she doesn't own shit.
jokes.setup: Discussing ownership percentages
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal that Dee owns nothing
jokes.explanation: Typical casual cruelty toward Dee. The matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

dialogue.line: Frank owned 51% of Paddy's Pub and we own 46%. Well, we did, not-not Dee. She-she doesn't own shit.

jokes.text: Yeah, I know. Goddamn it.
jokes.setup: Being told she owns nothing
jokes.punchline: Dee's resigned acceptance of being excluded
jokes.explanation: Dee's defeated acceptance is funny but simple. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, I know. Goddamn it.

jokes.text: What the hell is a Franquito? It says Franquito owns three percent of Paddy's Pub?
jokes.setup: Reading ownership documents
jokes.punchline: Discovery of mysterious owner 'Franquito'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later payoff. Creates mystery and intrigue.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What the hell is a Franquito? It says Franquito owns three percent of Paddy's Pub?

jokes.text: Oh, there-there's a throne? / Oh, sorry. No, I was just referring to the desk chair as the throne 'cause that's where the boss man sits.
jokes.setup: Mac getting excited about a throne
jokes.punchline: Dennis deflating the grandiosity to a simple desk chair
jokes.explanation: Good deflation humor. Mac's disappointment at the mundane reality is relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, there-there's a throne?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, sorry. No, I was just referring to the desk chair as the throne 'cause that's where the boss man sits.

jokes.text: Ugh, gross. That chair smells like Frank's butt. / It's not actually Frank's Frank's butt; I think it's a combination of all of our butt smells, you know? / That's worse.
jokes.setup: Discussing the chair smelling like Frank's butt
jokes.punchline: Revelation it's everyone's butt smells, which is worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure. Making it worse by making it everyone's contribution.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Ugh, gross. That chair smells like Frank's butt.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's not actually Frank's Frank's butt; I think it's a combination of all of our butt smells, you know?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's worse.

jokes.text: You think we're gonna give the shares to the man who wants to pull a mutant from a mystery hole in the bar and live with him?
jokes.setup: Mac wanting control of the bar
jokes.punchline: Dennis listing Mac's ridiculous mutant plan
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage. Highlighting Mac's absurd priorities.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You think we're gonna give the shares to the man who wants to pull a mutant from a mystery hole in the bar and live with him?

jokes.text: Paddy's Succession Plan, remember? / Oh, shit! Right. / Yes, right! Paddy's Succession Plan.
jokes.setup: Need to determine bar ownership
jokes.punchline: Sudden remembrance of their drunken succession plan
jokes.explanation: Good setup for absurd legal document reveal. The gang's fake formality is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Paddy's Succession Plan, remember?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, shit! Right.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yes, right! Paddy's Succession Plan.

jokes.text: We drank, like, three bottles of tequila and then we started talking about our legacy and how important that was to us.
jokes.setup: Explaining the succession plan
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was written while extremely drunk
jokes.explanation: Perfect gang behavior. Their serious legal planning while blackout drunk is very them.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We drank, like, three bottles of tequila and then we started talking about our legacy and how important that was to us.

jokes.text: Oh! Got to have a legacy. / Oh, here we go. / Oh, oh! Yeah, okay. / On a paper plate, huh?
jokes.setup: Discussing their important legacy document
jokes.punchline: The 'important' document is written on a paper plate
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag. The contrast between their serious tone and paper plate document is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh! Got to have a legacy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, here we go.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, oh! Yeah, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: On a paper plate, huh?

jokes.text: Paddy's succession plan. Paddy's Pub shall secure our legacy here, henceforth and forevermore throughout the birthright of our firstborn kin. Who had the pen here? It makes sense. Don't be a bitch.
jokes.setup: Reading their formal legal document
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-legal language ending with 'Don't be a bitch'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between formal legal language and crude addendum. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Paddy's succession plan. Paddy's Pub shall secure our legacy here, henceforth and forevermore throughout the birthright of our firstborn kin. Who had the pen here? It makes sense. Don't be a bitch.

jokes.text: 'Trundle,' written in blood. / Oh, yeah, I was going by Trundle at the time, wasn't I? / No, no, no. You were trying to write 'Charlie' and you wrote 'Trundle.'
jokes.setup: Charlie's signature on the document
jokes.punchline: Charlie trying to retcon his misspelling as an intentional name change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment. His attempt to save face about the misspelling is beautifully in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: 'Trundle,' written in blood.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, I was going by Trundle at the time, wasn't I?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, no, no. You were trying to write 'Charlie' and you wrote 'Trundle.'

jokes.text: No, I was going by 'Trundle the Great.' / You came up with that after you miswrote it.
jokes.setup: Charlie defending his 'Trundle' signature
jokes.punchline: Charlie doubling down with an even more ridiculous backstory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Charlie's delusion. Mac calling him out grounds it perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, I was going by 'Trundle the Great.'

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You came up with that after you miswrote it.

jokes.text: That's a binding contract. Okay? You wrote it in blood. That's gonna hold up in a court of law and I know when to pick a side. I'm siding with Dennis and Dee.
jokes.setup: Discussing the paper plate document's legitimacy
jokes.punchline: Mac's confident legal analysis of blood on paper plate
jokes.explanation: Mac's confident legal ignorance is perfect. His immediate side-switching is classic opportunism.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's a binding contract. Okay? You wrote it in blood. That's gonna hold up in a court of law and I know when to pick a side. I'm siding with Dennis and Dee.

jokes.text: Wait, hold on a second. You guys aren't even actually Frank's kid. Your Bruce Mathis' kid, all right? And I technically might be Frank's kid.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Frank's succession
jokes.punchline: Charlie suddenly bringing up paternity confusion
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven plot turn. Charlie's paternity obsession driving the story.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Wait, hold on a second. You guys aren't even actually Frank's kid. Your Bruce Mathis' kid, all right? And I technically might be Frank's kid.

jokes.text: Charlie, I pledge my allegiance to you. / No, no, no, no. / Wait a second, no. But Frank is a Reynolds and we are Reynolds. / It was always you guys. I tricked him, I tricked him. It was always you.
jokes.setup: Mac switching to Charlie's side
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately switching back and admitting he was lying
jokes.explanation: Mac's transparent opportunism and immediate betrayal is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie, I pledge my allegiance to you.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Wait a second, no. But Frank is a Reynolds and we are Reynolds.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It was always you guys. I tricked him, I tricked him. It was always you.

jokes.text: Heyo! There's my guy! Hey, I got a piping hot Grilled Frank for you, okay? I got the sausage, the Spam, the bacon. I got it wrapped in a jelly pancake and cooked with a stick of butter.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to be nice to Frank
jokes.punchline: The horrifically unhealthy 'Grilled Frank' sandwich description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie food creation. The naming and ingredients are beautifully disgusting.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Heyo! There's my guy! Hey, I got a piping hot Grilled Frank for you, okay? I got the sausage, the Spam, the bacon. I got it wrapped in a jelly pancake and cooked with a stick of butter.

jokes.text: I don't want that, Charlie. I'm trying to get in shape. I read all about this phenomenon that guys' bodies immediately turn to shit the second they retire.
jokes.setup: Charlie offering unhealthy food
jokes.punchline: Frank's sudden health consciousness based on retirement fears
jokes.explanation: Good character development. Frank's paranoia about retirement effects is relatable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I don't want that, Charlie. I'm trying to get in shape. I read all about this phenomenon that guys' bodies immediately turn to shit the second they retire.

jokes.text: Are you, like, um... my dad? / What? / Are, like... are you the-the father of me and shit?
jokes.setup: Charlie wanting to ask Frank something
jokes.punchline: Charlie's awkward, childlike way of asking about paternity
jokes.explanation: Charlie's childlike phrasing of a serious question is perfectly awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Are you, like, um... my dad?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Are, like... are you the-the father of me and shit?

jokes.text: We've-we've been over this. / Yeah. / Your mother's a giant whore.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about paternity
jokes.punchline: Frank's blunt, cruel dismissal
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual cruelty is perfectly in character. Great economy of language.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: We've-we've been over this.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Your mother's a giant whore.

jokes.text: I came up with a really great idea, dude. / Yeah? / I'm gonna play both sides. / Why would you tell me that?
jokes.setup: Mac sharing his strategy
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately revealing his secret plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac stupidity. Charlie's incredulous response sells it perfectly. Becomes a running gag.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I came up with a really great idea, dude.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm gonna play both sides.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Why would you tell me that?

jokes.text: Probably shouldn't 'cause if you're trying to keep a secret from me, well, now I know. / I should've... Should I tell them? / No, I don't think you should tell either side 'cause if you try to play both sides and they both know, you're not playing anybody.
jokes.setup: Mac revealing his strategy
jokes.punchline: Charlie having to explain basic logic to Mac
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mac's incompetence. Charlie being the voice of reason is absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Probably shouldn't 'cause if you're trying to keep a secret from me, well, now I know.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I should've... Should I tell them?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, I don't think you should tell either side 'cause if you try to play both sides and they both know, you're not playing anybody.

jokes.text: So I am prepared to take a blood oath with you. / Oh, my God! Oh, my God! Oh, my God, dude! Oh, that was Frank's toe knife!
jokes.setup: Mac preparing to make a blood oath
jokes.punchline: Mac accidentally using Frank's dirty toe knife
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy and gross-out humor. The toe knife detail adds disgust factor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So I am prepared to take a blood oath with you.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, my God! Oh, my God! Oh, my God, dude! Oh, that was Frank's toe knife!

jokes.text: No, I'm not gonna touch your blood. / Do you have something I could... I got to close this wound. / Here, just plug it up. / That sock's probably gonna get it more infected, though.
jokes.setup: Mac bleeding from the toe knife
jokes.punchline: Charlie offering a dirty sock and noting it will cause infection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the gross-out situation. Charlie's casual attitude about infection is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, I'm not gonna touch your blood.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Do you have something I could... I got to close this wound.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Here, just plug it up.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That sock's probably gonna get it more infected, though.

jokes.text: I don't know who that is. What I do know is I came to work this morning to find a very large bucket of blood in my lab.
jokes.setup: Gang asking about paternity test results
jokes.punchline: Doctor's matter-of-fact description of finding a bucket of blood
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery. The casual mention of a blood bucket is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: I don't know who that is. What I do know is I came to work this morning to find a very large bucket of blood in my lab.

jokes.text: Also, there's traces of more than four individuals in here and at least one animal. Was there blood in the bucket before? / It is a blood bucket, so yeah, there was old blood.
jokes.setup: Doctor analyzing the blood sample
jokes.punchline: Revelation they used a bucket that already had old blood and animal blood
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and Charlie's casual 'blood bucket' explanation is beautifully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Also, there's traces of more than four individuals in here and at least one animal. Was there blood in the bucket before?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It is a blood bucket, so yeah, there was old blood.

jokes.text: All right, Dee, the bar is finally ours. / Let's talk redecoration. / Well, I'm thinking chrome, and I'm thinking chrome everywhere.
jokes.setup: Planning their takeover of the bar
jokes.punchline: Dee's single-minded obsession with chrome everywhere
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Dee's tacky aesthetic preferences are perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All right, Dee, the bar is finally ours.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Let's talk redecoration.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, I'm thinking chrome, and I'm thinking chrome everywhere.

jokes.text: The other thing that's important to me is a proper dance floor. That way, any time I want to just pop a boogie, I can do it wherever I am, and it won't be weird, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Dee explaining her renovation plans
jokes.punchline: Dee's delusional belief that a dance floor will make her dancing less weird
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee delusion. Her belief that context will fix her awful dancing is spot-on.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: The other thing that's important to me is a proper dance floor. That way, any time I want to just pop a boogie, I can do it wherever I am, and it won't be weird, you know what I mean?

jokes.text: All right, you know what, you've been doing this all day. / Why are you talking like that? / I'm just being... I'm being arch.
jokes.setup: Dee talking in a strange voice
jokes.punchline: Dee's explanation that she's being 'arch'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy. Dee's pretentious explanation for bad acting is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: All right, you know what, you've been doing this all day.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Why are you talking like that?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm just being... I'm being arch.

jokes.text: Well, because we're being devious and we're plotting, and I just wanted to make sure that it was clear that we were devious and plotting.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking why she's being arch
jokes.punchline: Dee's overly literal explanation of indicating their deviousness
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about bad acting. Dee's overthinking of performance is relatable.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Well, because we're being devious and we're plotting, and I just wanted to make sure that it was clear that we were devious and plotting.

jokes.text: Dennis, Dee, quickly-- we don't have much time. Charlie and I doctored a paternity test to make it look like Frank is Charlie's dad. But the tests were actually inconclusive; there was too much blood in the bucket to tell. The reason I'm telling you all this is because I'm playing both sides so that I always come out on top.
jokes.setup: Mac approaching Dennis and Dee with information
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately revealing his entire strategy and betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Mac's incompetence. His complete transparency defeats his own scheme.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dennis, Dee, quickly-- we don't have much time. Charlie and I doctored a paternity test to make it look like Frank is Charlie's dad. But the tests were actually inconclusive; there was too much blood in the bucket to tell. The reason I'm telling you all this is because I'm playing both sides so that I always come out on top.

jokes.text: Number one, um, never tell one side that you're playing both sides. / Yeah. / And number two, if you are gonna play both sides, don't give away the information before you get what you want.
jokes.setup: Mac revealing his strategy
jokes.punchline: Dennis and Dee having to explain basic strategy to Mac
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff. Dennis's exasperated explanation highlights Mac's incompetence.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Number one, um, never tell one side that you're playing both sides.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: And number two, if you are gonna play both sides, don't give away the information before you get what you want.

jokes.text: You're still telling us that you're playing both sides, so... / Uh... Okay, well, what if I make a blood oath... uh, allegiance to you guys?
jokes.setup: Dennis pointing out Mac's continued error
jokes.punchline: Mac's solution being another blood oath
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback. Mac's one-note solution is perfectly dumb.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You're still telling us that you're playing both sides, so...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Uh... Okay, well, what if I make a blood oath... uh, allegiance to you guys?

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, here, I'll just get it again. / Oh! / Oh, shit! / Jesus, dude, that was the lime knife.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to reopen his wound for another blood oath
jokes.punchline: Mac using an even worse knife (lime knife)
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalation. The lime knife being worse adds to the grossness.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, here, I'll just get it again.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, shit!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Jesus, dude, that was the lime knife.

jokes.text: Your head of security just choked himself out.
jokes.setup: Mac attempting to choke Charlie out but failing
jokes.punchline: Dennis's matter-of-fact observation that Mac choked himself out
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and economy. The absurdity of Mac's incompetence is captured perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Your head of security just choked himself out.

jokes.text: I forgot to dye my hair this morning. / What's up with your teeth? / I was eating some of Deandra's pyramid scheme berries. I was trying to get healthy.
jokes.setup: Frank looking different
jokes.punchline: Frank's gray hair and stained teeth from health food
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag and character consistency. Frank's health obsession backfiring is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I forgot to dye my hair this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What's up with your teeth?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I was eating some of Deandra's pyramid scheme berries. I was trying to get healthy.

jokes.text: Are you okay? / What are... W-What's happening? / I'm... I'm being arch. / Oh. Oh. / We're doing a thing together, so you know...
jokes.setup: Dee trying to be conspiratorial with Charlie
jokes.punchline: Charlie's confusion at Dee's arch performance
jokes.explanation: Good callback but less impactful. The arch joke is wearing thin.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Are you okay?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What are... W-What's happening?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I'm... I'm being arch.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh. Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: We're doing a thing together, so you know...

jokes.text: Oh, my God, so much blood! Oh, so much blood, I think we hit an artery, Dee, look at this. / Is he okay? / No, he's okay, you know, the duct tape'll stop it.
jokes.setup: Taking blood sample from Frank
jokes.punchline: Massive bleeding treated casually with duct tape
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their medical incompetence. Charlie's casual attitude is darkly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, my God, so much blood! Oh, so much blood, I think we hit an artery, Dee, look at this.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Is he okay?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, he's okay, you know, the duct tape'll stop it.

jokes.text: Can't imagine it was gonna be a very good sample anyway-- there are quite a few pickles floating around in that bag.
jokes.setup: They contaminate the blood sample
jokes.punchline: Dee notices pickles in their blood collection bag
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail of pickles contaminating blood sample. Connects to Charlie's sandwich offering.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Can't imagine it was gonna be a very good sample anyway-- there are quite a few pickles floating around in that bag.

jokes.text: Worst is first, dude, worst is first. / What are you talking about, man? / Dee is conspiring against you. Charlie figured it out. 'Worst is first.'
jokes.setup: Mac revealing information to Dennis
jokes.punchline: The cryptic 'worst is first' phrase as major intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good callback and Mac's dramatic delivery of the cryptic phrase works well.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Worst is first, dude, worst is first.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What are you talking about, man?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Dee is conspiring against you. Charlie figured it out. 'Worst is first.'

jokes.text: 'Worst is first.' My mom used to say that to me all the time because I was first and she thought I was the worst. / Holy shit, that's terrible, but that's good for us.
jokes.setup: Explaining the 'worst is first' phrase
jokes.punchline: Revelation of Dee's mother's cruel treatment and Charlie's callous response
jokes.explanation: Great dark humor. The casual cruelty from both mother and Charlie is perfectly awful.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: 'Worst is first.' My mom used to say that to me all the time because I was first and she thought I was the worst.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Holy shit, that's terrible, but that's good for us.

jokes.text: Oh... oh, my God. That is crazy. It's the smell of a thousand butts.
jokes.setup: Mac smelling the chair
jokes.punchline: Escalating the butt smell to 'a thousand butts'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag. Simple but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh... oh, my God. That is crazy. It's the smell of a thousand butts.

jokes.text: You idiots. Did you not think that I had anticipated this? While you were running around town like lunatics, chopping your hands to bits, I did a little bit of research. Turns out Franquito isn't a shell corporation after all. He's a person.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinking they have the upper hand
jokes.punchline: Dennis revealing Franquito is a real person he found
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and plot twist. Dennis's smugness and the absurd solution are perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You idiots. Did you not think that I had anticipated this? While you were running around town like lunatics, chopping your hands to bits, I did a little bit of research. Turns out Franquito isn't a shell corporation after all. He's a person.

jokes.text: Franquito! / Ven acá! / Holy shit! / Whoa!
jokes.setup: Dennis revealing Franquito
jokes.punchline: A real person who looks like Frank appears
jokes.explanation: Great visual reveal. The absurdity of finding a Frank doppelganger is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Franquito!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Ven acá!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Holy shit!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Whoa!

jokes.text: Yeah, he doesn't speak any English, by the way, so don't try talking to him. But we worked something out. Yeah, Frank's shares go to him, 'cause he's the firstborn... and then Franquito signs to me.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains his master plan
jokes.punchline: The elaborate scheme to use Franquito as a proxy
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis overcomplicated scheme. Using language barrier as part of manipulation is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, he doesn't speak any English, by the way, so don't try talking to him. But we worked something out. Yeah, Frank's shares go to him, 'cause he's the firstborn... and then Franquito signs to me.

jokes.text: My one true child. Come closer. / Oh! / You give me so much joy right now.
jokes.setup: Frank meeting his supposed son
jokes.punchline: Frank's immediate emotional connection to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Frank's desperate need for family connection is touching and pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: My one true child. Come closer.
Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You give me so much joy right now.

jokes.text: When a man is no longer needed in the tribe, his body betrays him. / When he has no purpose, the blood seems to drain from his body, leaving him a shell of the man he once was.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his condition
jokes.punchline: Frank's pseudo-philosophical explanation for retirement symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good Frank pseudo-wisdom. His dramatic self-pity is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: When a man is no longer needed in the tribe, his body betrays him. When he has no purpose, the blood seems to drain from his body, leaving him a shell of the man he once was.

jokes.text: Speaking of number two... / Oh! / Dude. / Oh! / Damn it! Come on. I just cleaned that.
jokes.setup: Frank mentioning Franquito as his 'number two'
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately defecating himself
jokes.explanation: Simple pun setup with gross payoff. Not sophisticated but gets a reaction.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Speaking of number two...

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dude.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Damn it! Come on. I just cleaned that.

jokes.text: That's Frank's butt smell. That is it. I told you. Oh, no! That is the exact smell of the chair.
jokes.setup: Frank defecating
jokes.punchline: Mac realizing this is the source of the chair smell
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to running gag. Mac's realization connects the dots nicely.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's Frank's butt smell. That is it. I told you. Oh, no! That is the exact smell of the chair.

jokes.text: All right, look, guys, it's very simple. Seven years ago, I walked into a Wahwah, and I saw this guy, and I thought, 'Holy, shit! That guy looks just like Frank, but, you know, Mexican.'
jokes.setup: Everyone believing Franquito is Frank's son
jokes.punchline: Dennis revealing the entire elaborate scam started at a Wawa
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection reveal. The casual racism and elaborate long con starting at Wawa is perfect Dennis.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All right, look, guys, it's very simple. Seven years ago, I walked into a Wahwah, and I saw this guy, and I thought, 'Holy, shit! That guy looks just like Frank, but, you know, Mexican.'

jokes.text: And then I came up with a plan where I could make Frank believe that he had a son with our maid Josefina all those years ago, and I could bilk him out of a shitload of money under the guise that the money was all going to his firstborn son, Franquito, when, in fact, the money was going to me.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his scam
jokes.punchline: The elaborate nature and long duration of his con game
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Dennis's sociopathy. The casual admission of years of fraud is perfectly awful.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And then I came up with a plan where I could make Frank believe that he had a son with our maid Josefina all those years ago, and I could bilk him out of a shitload of money under the guise that the money was all going to his firstborn son, Franquito, when, in fact, the money was going to me.

jokes.text: Yeah, but that's, like... incredibly devious, dude. I mean, you've been screwing Frank for years? / Yeah. I mean, you guys would have done that, too, right, if you'd thought of it?
jokes.setup: Mac being shocked at Dennis's deviousness
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual assumption everyone would commit the same fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment. His complete lack of moral awareness and projection is spot-on.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah, but that's, like... incredibly devious, dude. I mean, you've been screwing Frank for years?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. I mean, you guys would have done that, too, right, if you'd thought of it?

jokes.text: Because Franquito's not your son. I made it all up. / What?! How?!
jokes.setup: Frank's emotional reunion with his son
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals the entire thing was fake
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot twist that recontextualizes everything. Frank's betrayed reaction is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Because Franquito's not your son. I made it all up.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: What?! How?!

jokes.text: No, Franquito. We're done here. / Can I get my five bucks? / Yes, you can get your five bucks. Goddamn, you're a greedy bastard.
jokes.setup: The elaborate Franquito reveal and scheme
jokes.punchline: Franquito was paid five dollars for the performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation ending. The elaborate scheme costing five dollars and Dennis calling Franquito greedy is beautifully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, Franquito. We're done here.

dialogue.speaker: Franquito
dialogue.line: Can I get my five bucks?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yes, you can get your five bucks. Goddamn, you're a greedy bastard.
